agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Direct SQL injection via unsanitized user input in query string interpolation"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via f-string query construction","risk":"Attacker can execute arbitrary SQL — full database read, modification, or deletion — by injecting payloads through customer_id or status_filter query parameters","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (query construction)","reproduction":"GET /api/deliveries?customer_id=' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --&status_filter=any — the f-string interpolates this directly into the query, producing: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --' AND status = 'any'","fix":"Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter))","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security: no user input should ever be interpolated into SQL strings"},{"title":"No input validation on public-facing parameters","risk":"Even with parameterized queries, missing validation means bogus or oversized inputs hit the database unnecessarily, enabling enumeration or DoS via expensive queries","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function signature — customer_id and status_filter accepted as raw strings with no validation","reproduction":"GET /api/deliveries?customer_id=&status_filter= — empty strings return unintended result sets. Extremely long strings consume memory and DB resources.","fix":"Validate customer_id against expected format (e.g., UUID regex) and status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., 'pending', 'delivered', 'cancelled') before executing the query. Return 400 for invalid input.","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Input validation: all public API parameters must be validated before use"},{"title":"SELECT * returns unbounded result set with no pagination","risk":"A valid customer with thousands of deliveries (or an attacker using injection to widen the WHERE clause) triggers an unbounded SELECT that can OOM the application server or saturate the DB connection pool","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function, line 2 (SELECT *) and missing LIMIT clause","reproduction":"A wholesale/enterprise customer_id with 500k delivery records — fetchall() loads every row into memory at once","fix":"Add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination (e.g., LIMIT 100), return only required columns instead of SELECT *, and consider streaming results via fetchmany() or server-side cursors for large sets","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Performance: API responses must be bounded and paginated"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No authorization check — horizontal privilege escalation","description":"Any authenticated user can query any other customer's deliveries by supplying an arbitrary customer_id. The function does not verify the caller is authorized to view the requested customer's data.","location":"search_deliveries function — no ownership check before query execution"},{"title":"SELECT * couples API response to table schema","description":"Adding or reordering columns in the deliveries table silently changes the API response shape, potentially leaking internal fields (e.g., internal_notes, cost_price) to external consumers.","location":"search_deliveries function, line 3"}],"suggestions":["Add structured logging for query execution (without logging parameter values) to support incident response","Return a typed dataclass or Pydantic model instead of raw tuples from fetchall()","Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not already exist to support this query pattern"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code is exploitable the moment it is reachable from the public internet. A single crafted request can compromise the entire database.","rollback_capability":"Rollback of the deployment is straightforward, but if the SQLi is exploited before rollback, data may already be exfiltrated or destroyed — rollback does not undo that damage.","backward_compatibility":"Fixing to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers. No API contract change required.","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or metrics around query execution — exploitation would be invisible without WAF or DB audit logs.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["auth_security: no authorization boundary — any caller can access any customer's data","auth_security: SQL injection allows full database compromise"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Revert deployment immediately. If any exploitation is suspected, rotate DB credentials, audit query logs, and assess data integrity."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":99,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is textbook-certain from the code","Unbounded SELECT * with fetchall() is visible in the code","No input validation is visible in the code"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether a WAF or ORM layer sits in front of this function is unknown but irrelevant — defense in depth requires the function itself to be safe"],"assumptions":["db_conn.execute() sends the query string directly to the database engine","No upstream middleware sanitizes these parameters before they reach this function"]}}
